title: Create a Real Coffee Mug
description: Fusion tutorial player: revolve + shell mug body, then sweep a handle on an offset plane.

steps.title: Start Sketch on XZ Plane (Set the Axis)
steps.instruction: Create a new sketch on the XZ plane, then draw a vertical construction line for the revolve axis.
steps.detailedText: We’ll sketch a half-profile and revolve it around the centerline to form a clean, symmetric mug body.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Choosing the correct sketch plane ensures the revolve axis aligns properly with the mug centerline. This prevents misalignment errors and simplifies downstream modeling operations.

tips: Use the XZ plane so your revolve axis is vertical (centerline).
Make the axis line a **construction** line so it doesn’t create extra profiles.

steps.title: Sketch the Mug Half‑Profile (Outer Wall)
steps.instruction: On one side of the centerline, sketch the outer mug profile: ~95 mm tall, base radius ~34 mm, rim radius ~38 mm (slight flare). Add a small bottom thickness (~4–6 mm) in the sketch.
steps.detailedText: A smooth, slightly tapered silhouette looks realistic and makes Shell more reliable. Keep the profile as one closed region.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: A tapered silhouette improves ergonomics and realism. Smooth transitions reduce geometry failures in Shell/Fillet later and keep the timeline robust.

tips: Keep everything on ONE side of the centerline (axis).
Use tangent arcs where the base meets the wall to avoid sharp corners.
Add key dimensions now (height, base/rim radius) so edits stay stable.

qcChecks.text: Profile is a single closed region

fusionActions.text: Draw a closed outer profile (one side of axis).

steps.title: Close Profile and Finish Sketch
steps.instruction: Ensure the profile region is shaded (closed), then click Finish Sketch.
steps.detailedText: The shaded region confirms Fusion can revolve a watertight solid.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: A closed profile is required to generate a watertight revolve body. This prevents downstream failures when applying Shell and Fillets.

tips: If it does not shade, look for tiny gaps or overlapping endpoints.
Avoid extra stray sketch entities near the axis.

fusionActions.text: Confirm the profile is closed (shaded), then finish sketch.

steps.title: Revolve Outer Body
steps.instruction: Solid > Create > Revolve. Select the closed profile and revolve 360° around the construction centerline (or Y-axis). Operation: New Body.
steps.detailedText: This produces a clean outer solid. We hollow it next using Shell so thickness stays editable.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Revolve is the most stable way to create circular-symmetric objects. Revolving the outer form first simplifies later edits and avoids sketching both inner and outer walls.

tips: Select the shaded profile region, not just sketch lines.
If the axis intersects the profile region, fix the sketch so it stays on one side.

fusionActions.text: Revolve profile 360° around Y-axis → New Body.

steps.title: Shell the Mug
steps.instruction: Solid > Modify > Shell. Select the top face (opening) and set thickness to 3 mm.
steps.detailedText: Shell hollows the mug while keeping a uniform wall thickness that is realistic and print-friendly.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Shell creates consistent wall thickness without requiring a second inner sketch profile. This improves editability and reduces sketch complexity, making the model more robust.

tips: If Shell fails, reduce thickness to 2 mm and try again.
Very tight base curvature can cause Shell failures—smooth the base in the sketch if needed.

fusionActions.text: Shell the mug from the top face at 3mm thickness.

steps.title: Round the Rim (Fillet)
steps.instruction: Solid > Modify > Fillet. Select the top inner edge and top outer edge. Radius ~2 mm.
steps.detailedText: A rounded rim improves comfort and looks more premium.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Fillets improve ergonomics and reduce stress concentration. A rounded rim also avoids thin sharp edges that may chip or print poorly.

tips: Start at 1 mm if 2 mm fails, then increase gradually.
Select both inner and outer rim edges for a comfortable lip.

fusionActions.text: Fillet rim edges to improve comfort.

steps.title: Create an Offset Plane for the Handle
steps.instruction: Construct > Offset Plane. Pick the YZ plane and offset it by ~38 mm (about the rim radius) so the sketch plane sits on the mug’s side.
steps.detailedText: Sketching the handle path on an offset plane keeps it aligned to the mug side and makes the sweep attach cleanly.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: The handle path determines usability. Proper clearance prevents knuckle collision and ensures the handle is comfortable and functional.

tips: If your mug radius is different, offset by your actual outer radius.
Name the plane 'Handle Plane' in the browser to stay organized.

fusionActions.text: Draw a handle path arc with enough clearance for fingers.

steps.title: Sketch the Handle Path (3‑Point Arc)
steps.instruction: Start a sketch on the Handle Plane. Draw a smooth 3‑point arc for the handle path. Endpoints should touch the mug wall; arc should bulge outward for ~30 mm finger clearance.
steps.detailedText: This path controls comfort and clearance. Keep it smooth and avoid sharp kinks.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: A closed, centered profile prevents sweep twisting and ensures a clean join to the mug body. The size affects comfort and strength.

tips: Make endpoints coincident with the mug outer wall silhouette (project edge if needed).
A gentle C‑shape feels better than a tight curve.

fusionActions.text: Draw a closed handle profile (about 11x8mm) and finish the sketch.

steps.title: Create Profile at Path Start (Plane Along Path)
steps.instruction: Construct > Plane Along Path at the start of the handle arc. Sketch on that plane and draw a centered profile ~11 mm × 8 mm (rounded rectangle or oval). Finish sketch.
steps.detailedText: A perpendicular profile prevents sweep twisting and keeps the handle thickness consistent.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Sweep preserves a consistent cross-section along the curved path. Join ensures the handle becomes part of the mug for printing and structural integrity.

tips: Keep the profile centered on the path start point.
If the sweep twists, re-check that the profile plane is **perpendicular** to the path.

fusionActions.text: Sweep profile along path and set operation to Join.

steps.title: Sweep Handle (Join) + Blend Joints
steps.instruction: Solid > Create > Sweep: select the handle profile, then the arc path. Operation: Join. Then fillet the handle‑to‑body joint edges (start ~3 mm).
steps.detailedText: Joining makes the mug printable as one solid. Fillets strengthen the joint and improve the look.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Filleting the handle joint reduces stress concentration and improves durability. It also improves aesthetics, making the handle look designed rather than attached.

tips: If Join fails, ensure both handle ends intersect the mug body (extend the path slightly if needed).
Apply smaller fillets first, then increase gradually.

fusionActions.text: Blend handle joints using Fillet for strength and realism.
